title: Hierarchy of Granularity Levels in Multiple Granularity Locking
description: A tree diagram showing the hierarchical levels of data granularity, from the entire database down to a single field, and illustrating how intention locks (IS, IX) are used at higher levels to request shared or exclusive locks at finer levels.
relationships: A 'Database' node is the parent of multiple 'Area' nodes. An 'Area' node is the parent of multiple 'File' nodes. A 'File' node is the parent of multiple 'Record' nodes. A 'Record' node is the parent of multiple 'Field' nodes. Intention-Shared (IS) and Intention-Exclusive (IX) locks are acquired on parent nodes before requesting S or X locks on their children.
